idea: GitHub Actions pipeline for a containerized Python service
why_this_project_matters: This project gives teams a simple but useful CI baseline with linting, tests, and image build validation on every push.
architecture: GitHub Actions runs a single workflow that checks out the repository, installs dependencies, runs unit tests, and builds a Docker image. The workflow stops before deployment, keeping the first version focused on confidence-building automation.
steps: Commit the sample application and workflow into a GitHub repository.
Push a branch and confirm the CI workflow runs linting, tests, and the image build.
Break a test intentionally to see the failure mode before restoring the passing state.
Add branch protection once the workflow is stable.
Extend the pipeline with image publishing or deployment stages when the team is ready.
